explanation: Especially on the occasion of / Limited to
exampleSentence: 彼に限って約束を破ることはない。
exampleTranslation: He would never break a promise.

explanation: If one could (do something) / If by any chance
exampleSentence: できるものなら、もう一度やり直したい。
exampleTranslation: If it were possible, I would want to start over again.

explanation: If someone were to (do something), then (bad result)
exampleSentence: 遅刻しようものなら、社長に怒られる。
exampleTranslation: If I were to be late, the president would get angry at me.

explanation: That is truly (something) / That is what I call
usageRules: Noun/な-Adjective + というものだ, Verb plain + というものだ
exampleSentence: 最後まで諦めないことこそ、本当の勇気というものだ。
exampleTranslation: Never giving up until the end is what true courage is.

explanation: It is not necessarily the case that
usageRules: Verb plain + というものではない
exampleSentence: 給料が高ければいいというものではない。
exampleTranslation: It is not necessarily good just because the salary is high.

exampleSentence: 忙しいにしても、連絡ぐらいはできるだろう。
exampleTranslation: Even if you're busy, you could at least contact me.

usageRules: Noun/Verb plain + にしろ／にせよ
exampleSentence: 理由はどうであれ、遅刻したことに変わりはない。
exampleTranslation: Whatever the reason, the fact that you were late remains unchanged.

exampleSentence: 経験を問わず、意欲のある方を募集します。
exampleTranslation: We are recruiting motivated individuals regardless of experience.

exampleSentence: 味はともかく、量は十分だった。
exampleTranslation: Setting aside the taste, the portion was sufficient.

exampleSentence: 昨年の反省を踏まえて、今年の計画を立てる。
exampleTranslation: We will make this year's plan based on last year's reflections.

exampleSentence: 天候にかかわらず、試合は予定通り行います。
exampleTranslation: Regardless of the weather, the match will be held as scheduled.

usageRules: Noun/な-Adjective + である + にもかかわらず, Verb/い-Adjective plain + にもかかわらず
exampleSentence: 雨が降っているにもかかわらず、彼は傘をささずに歩いている。
exampleTranslation: Despite the rain, he is walking without an umbrella.

exampleSentence: 新しいアプリをインストールしたものの、使い方がよくわからない。
exampleTranslation: Although I installed the new app, I don't really understand how to use it.

exampleSentence: 勉強しなければと思いつつも、ついスマホを見てしまう。
exampleTranslation: Even though I know I should study, I end up looking at my phone.

explanation: Even if / No matter how (with little effect)
exampleSentence: 今さら後悔したところで、結果は変わらない。
exampleTranslation: Even if I regret it now, the result won't change.

exampleSentence: 今回の成功は、チーム全員の努力の結果にほかならない。
exampleTranslation: This success is nothing but the result of the entire team's efforts.

exampleSentence: 彼はただの知人にすぎない。
exampleTranslation: He is merely an acquaintance.

usageRules: Noun/な-Adjective + である + に違いない, Verb/い-Adjective plain + に違いない
exampleSentence: あの雲の様子からすると、明日は雨に違いない。
exampleTranslation: Judging from those clouds, it must rain tomorrow.

explanation: Could possibly / Is liable to (negative outcome)
exampleSentence: そんなにスピードを出すと、事故を起こしかねない。
exampleTranslation: If you drive that fast, you could cause an accident.

exampleSentence: そのご要望にはお応えしかねます。
exampleTranslation: We are unable to accommodate that request.

usageRules: Verb plain + わけにはいかない
exampleSentence: 大事な会議があるので、休むわけにはいかない。
exampleTranslation: There is an important meeting, so I cannot afford to take the day off.

explanation: Cannot help but / Cannot refrain from
usageRules: Verb ない-form + ではいられない
exampleSentence: その話を聞いて、笑わないではいられなかった。
exampleTranslation: Upon hearing that story, I couldn't help but laugh.

exampleSentence: 皆様のご健康を心よりお祈りしてやみません。
exampleTranslation: I sincerely pray for your good health.

explanation: Probably not / Will not / Let's not
exampleSentence: こうなっては、もう二度と彼に会うことはあるまい。
exampleTranslation: At this point, I will probably never see him again.

explanation: Even if not / If not at least
exampleSentence: 全部はできないまでも、半分ぐらいは手伝える。
exampleTranslation: Even if I can't do all of it, I can help with at least half.

exampleSentence: マニュアルに沿って作業を進めてください。
exampleTranslation: Please proceed with the work in accordance with the manual.

usageRules: Noun + に伴って, Verb plain + に伴って
exampleSentence: 人口の増加に伴って、住宅問題が深刻化している。
exampleTranslation: Along with the population increase, the housing problem is becoming more serious.

exampleSentence: 技術が進歩するにつれて、私たちの生活は便利になった。
exampleTranslation: As technology advances, our lives have become more convenient.

explanation: Over a period of / Across a range of
exampleSentence: この会議は3時間にわたって行われた。
exampleTranslation: This meeting was held over a period of three hours.

usageRules: Noun + を通じて／を通して
exampleSentence: 日本人は四季を通じて様々な行事を楽しむ。
exampleTranslation: Japanese people enjoy various events throughout the four seasons.

usageRules: Noun + の + 際に, Verb plain/polite + 際に
exampleSentence: ご利用の際には、会員カードをご提示ください。
exampleTranslation: Please present your membership card when using the service.
